title: देवी माहात्म्यम् मध्यम चरित्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री देवी माहात्म्यम् - मध्यम चरित्रम् ॥

ॐ नमश्चण्डिकायै॥

ध्यानम् -
अक्षस्रक्परशुं गदेषुकुलिशं पद्मं धनुः कुण्डिकां
दण्डं शक्तिमसिं च चर्म जलजं घण्टां सुराभाजनम्।
शूलं पाशसुदर्शने च दधतीं हस्तैः प्रवालप्रभां
सेवे सैरिभमर्दिनीमिह महालक्ष्मीं सरोजस्थिताम्॥

ॐ ह्रीं देव्या हतं महिषासुरस्य सैन्यम् -

महिषासुरः सेनापतिर्देवानां जितवान्स्वयम्।
त्रैलोक्यं तेन संक्रान्तं देवा विष्णुं समाश्रयन्॥१॥

तेजः समस्तदेवानां वदनात्सम्बभूव ह।
ततः समस्तदेवानां तेजोराशिसमुद्भवाम्॥२॥

तां ददृशुः सुरा देवीं व्याप्तलोकत्रयां त्विषा।
शिवतेजः समुद्भूतं मुखमन्दरसुन्दरम्॥३॥

यामद्भिस्तु केशा विष्णुतेजसो बाहवः स्मृताः।
सौम्यतेजसमुद्भूतौ स्तनौ तेजो यमस्य च॥४॥

मध्यं प्रजापतेस्तेजः कटिपादौ वसुन्धरा।
ब्रह्मतेजोद्भवौ पादावङ्गुल्यर्कांशुतेजसः॥५॥

वसूनां च करांगुल्यः कौबेरं नासिकां तथा।
तस्यास्तु दन्ताः सम्भूताः प्राजापत्यं तथैव च॥६॥

नयनत्रयमुत्पन्नं तथा पावकसम्भवम्।
भ्रुवौ च सन्ध्ययोस्तेजः श्रवणावनिलस्य च॥७॥

अन्येषां चैव देवानां सम्भवस्तेजसां शिवा।
ततः समस्तदेवानां तेजोराशिसमुद्भवाम्॥८॥

तां विलोक्य मुदं प्रापुरमरा महिषार्दिताः।
शूलं शूलाद्विनिष्कृष्य ददौ तस्यै पिनाकधृक्॥९॥

चक्रं च दत्तवान् कृष्णः समुत्पाट्य स्वचक्रतः।
शंखं च वरुणः शक्तिं ददौ तस्यै हुताशनः॥१०॥

मारुतो दत्तवांश्चापं बाणपूर्णे तथेषुधी।
वज्रमिन्द्रः समुत्पाट्य कुलिशादमरेश्वरः॥११॥

ददौ तस्यै सहस्राक्षो घण्टामैरावताद्गजात्।
कालदण्डाद्यमो दण्डं पाशं चाम्बुपतिर्ददौ॥१२॥

प्रजापतिश्चाक्षमालां ददौ ब्रह्मा कमण्डलुम्।
समस्तरोमकूपेषु निजरश्मीन् दिवाकरः॥१३॥

कालश्च दत्तवान् खड्गं तस्याश्चर्म च निर्मलम्।
क्षीरोदश्चामलं हारमजरे च तथाम्बरे॥१४॥

चूडामणिं तथा दिव्यं कुण्डले कटकानि च।
अर्धचन्द्रं तथा शुभ्रं केयूरान् सर्ववाहुषु॥१५॥

नूपुरौ विमलौ तद्वद्ग्रैवेयकमनुत्तमम्।
अङ्गुलीयकरत्नानि समस्तास्वंगुलीषु च॥१६॥

विश्वकर्मा ददौ तस्यै परशुं चातिनिर्मलम्।
अस्त्राण्यनेकरूपाणि तथाभेद्यं च दंशनम्॥१७॥

सा देवी सर्वदेवानां तेजोभिः समलंकृता।
जहास तालजङ्घेशं महिषासुरसैनिकम्॥१८॥

ततो महिषासुरः महिषरूपेण धावित्वा
देवीं प्रति युद्धाय समागतः।
तं दृष्ट्वा देव्याभवत्कोपः प्रचण्डम्॥१९॥

सा क्षिप्त्वा तस्य वै पाशं महिषं तमबन्धयत्।
महिषं तं परित्यज्य सोऽभवत्सिंह एव हि॥२०॥

सिंहस्य नाशयामास शिरो देवी महासिना।
ततः खड्गधरो दैत्यो ददर्श चण्डिकाम्॥२१॥

ततस्तु महिषं रूपं पुनर्धृत्वा स दानवः।
त्रैलोक्यं सचराचरं क्षोभयामास ललितम्॥२२॥

तमभ्यधावत्कृद्धा सा चण्डिका तमरिन्दमम्।
पदा महिषमाक्रम्य शूलेनैनमताडयत्॥२३॥

ततः सोऽपि पदाक्रान्तो मुखात्स्वरूपमास्थितः।
अर्धनिष्क्रान्त एवासौ युध्यमानो महासुरः॥२४॥

तस्यापि नाशयामास शिरो देवी महासिना।
ततो हाहाकृतं सर्वं दैत्यसैन्यं ननाश तत्॥२५॥

प्रमुदिताः सुरगणाः समूहतुषुस्ततो देवाः।
स्तुवन्ति स्म स ताम्देवीं सह दिव्यैर्मुनीश्वरैः॥२६॥

॥ इति श्री मार्कण्डेयपुराणे देवीमाहात्म्ये मध्यमचरित्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Devi Mahatmyam - Madhyama Charitram ||

Om Namashchandikayai.

Dhyanam -
Aksha-srak-parashum Gadeshuku-lisham Padmam Dhanuh Kundikam
Dandam Shaktim Asim Cha Charma Jalajam Ghantam Surabhaajanam.
Shulam Pasha-sudarshane Cha Dadhatim Hastaih Pravala-prabham
Seve Sairibha-mardinim Iha Mahalakshmim Saroja-sthitam.

Om Hrim Devya Hatam Mahishasurasya Sainyam -

Mahishasurah Senapatir Devanam Jitavan Svayam.
Trailokyam Tena Samkrantam Deva Vishnum Samashrayat.||1||

Tejah Samasta-devanam Vadanat Sambabhuva Ha.
Tatah Samasta-devanam Tejo-rashi-samudbhavam.||2||

Tam Dadriushuh Sura Devim Vyapta-loka-trayam Tvisha.
Shiva-tejah Samudbhutam Mukham Andara-sundaram.||3||

Yamad-bhis Tu Kesha Vishnu-tejaso Bahavah Smritah.
Saumya-tejasam Udbhutau Stanau Tejo Yamasya Cha.||4||

Madhyam Prajapateh Tejah Kati-padau Vasundhara.
Brahma-tejodbhavau Padav Anguly-arkamshu-tejasah.||5||

Vasunam Cha Karangulayah Kauberam Nasikam Tatha.
Tasyas Tu Dantah Sambhutah Prajapatyam Tathaiva Cha.||6||

Nayana-trayam Utpannam Tatha Pavaka-sambhavam.
Bhruvau Cha Sandhyayos Tejah Shravanav Anilasya Cha.||7||

Anyesham Chaiva Devanam Sambhavas Tejasam Shiva.
Tatah Samasta-devanam Tejo-rashi-samudbhavam.||8||

Tam Vilokya Mudam Prapur Amara Mahishaarditah.
Shulam Shulad Vinishkrishya Dadau Tasyai Pinaka-dhrik.||9||

Chakram Cha Dattavan Krishnah Samutpatya Sva-chakratah.
Shanakham Cha Varunah Shaktim Dadau Tasyai Hutashanah.||10||

Maruto Dattavamsh Chapam Bana-purne Tatheshudhi.
Vajram Indrah Samutpatya Kulishad Amareshvarah.||11||

Dadau Tasyai Sahasrakshyo Ghantam Airavatad Gajat.
Kala-dandad Yamo Dandam Pasham Chambupati Dadau.||12||

Prajapatis Chaksha-malam Dadau Brahma Kamandulum.
Samasta-roma-kupeshu Nija-rashmin Divakarah.||13||

Kalash Cha Dattavan Khadgam Tasyash Charma Cha Nirmalam.
Kshirodash Chamalam Haram Ajare Cha Tathambare.||14||

Chuda-manim Tatha Divyam Kundale Katakani Cha.
Ardha-chandram Tatha Shubhram Keyuran Sarva-bahushu.||15||

Nupurau Vimalau Tadvad Graiveyakam Anuttamam.
Anguliyaka-ratnani Samastasv Angulishu Cha.||16||

Vishvakarma Dadau Tasyai Parashum Chati-nirmalam.
Astrany Aneka-rupani Tathabhedyam Cha Damshanam.||17||

Sa Devi Sarva-devanam Tejobhih Samalamkrita.
Jahasa Tala-janghesham Mahishasura-sainikam.||18||

Tato Mahishasurah Mahisha-rupena Dhavitva
Devim Prati Yuddhaya Samagatah.
Tam Drishtva Devyabhavat Kopah Prachandam.||19||

Sa Kshiptva Tasya Vai Pasham Mahisham Tam Abandhayat.
Mahisham Tam Parityajya So'bhavat Simha Eva Hi.||20||

Simhasya Nashayamasa Shiro Devi Mahasina.
Tatah Khadga-dharo Daityo Dadarsha Chandikam.||21||

Tatas Tu Mahisham Rupam Punar Dhritva Sa Danavah.
Trailokyam Sacharacharam Kshobhayamasa Lalitam.||22||

Tam Abhyadhavat Kruddha Sa Chandika Tam Arindamam.
Pada Mahisham Akramya Shulena Enam Atadayat.||23||

Tatah So'pi Padakranto Mukhat Svarupam Asthitah.
Ardha-nishkranta Evasau Yudhyamano Mahasurah.||24||

Tasyapi Nashayamasa Shiro Devi Mahasina.
Tato Hahakritam Sarvam Daitya-sainyam Nanasha Tat.||25||

Pramuditah Sura-ganah Samuha-tushus Tato Devah.
Stuvanti Sma Sa Tam Devim Saha Divyair Munishvaraih.||26||

|| Iti Shri Markandeya-purane Devi-mahatmye Madhyama-charitram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
महिषासुर ने देवताओं को जीतकर तीनों लोकों पर अधिकार कर लिया। पराजित देवताओं ने विष्णु की शरण ली।

तब समस्त देवताओं के तेज से एक अद्भुत देवी प्रकट हुईं। शिव के तेज से मुख, विष्णु के तेज से भुजाएँ, यम के तेज से स्तन, प्रजापति के तेज से मध्यभाग, पृथ्वी के तेज से कटि और पैर, ब्रह्मा के तेज से चरण, सूर्य के तेज से अंगुलियाँ बनीं।

शिव ने त्रिशूल, विष्णु ने चक्र, वरुण ने शंख, अग्नि ने शक्ति, वायु ने धनुष-बाण, इन्द्र ने वज्र, यम ने दण्ड, वरुण ने पाश, ब्रह्मा ने अक्षमाला और कमण्डलु, सूर्य ने किरणें, काल ने खड्ग और ढाल दी।

इस प्रकार सज्जित देवी ने महिषासुर की सेना का संहार किया। महिषासुर ने भैंसे, सिंह, हाथी आदि अनेक रूप धारण किए। अंत में देवी ने पैर से उसे दबाकर, जब वह महिषमुख से बाहर निकलने लगा, तब महान खड्ग से उसका शिर काट दिया। देवताओं और मुनियों ने देवी की स्तुति की।

viniyog.shloka: ॐ अस्य श्रीमध्यमचरित्रस्य विष्णुर्ऋषिः। उष्णिक् छन्दः। श्रीमहालक्ष्मीर्देवता। शाकम्भरी शक्तिः। दुर्गा बीजम्। वायुस्तत्त्वम्। यजुर्वेदः स्वरूपम्। श्रीमहालक्ष्मीप्रीत्यर्थे मध्यमचरित्रपाठे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Narrates the creation of Devi from combined divine energies of all gods
Recitation destroys rajas (passion, ego) and restores cosmic balance
Invokes victory over powerful enemies and seemingly invincible obstacles
Invokes the combined power of all deities through the Goddess
Central portion of Durga Saptashati essential for Navratri worship
Invokes the blessing of courage, strength, and divine weaponry of protection
description: The Devi Mahatmyam Madhyama Charitra, known in Sanskrit as देवी माहात्म्यम् मध्यम चरित्रम्, is a sacred prayer in praise of Goddess Durga. Drawn from Markandeya Purana (Devi Mahatmyam / Durga Saptashati), this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Regular recitation is believed to invoke the combined power of all deities through the Goddess, bestow courage, strength, and divine weaponry of protection, and recitation destroys rajas (passion, ego) and restore cosmic balance. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Friday (Shukravar) and during Navratri. The text contains 26 verses and takes around 25 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Devi Mahatmyam Madhyama Charitra in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Devi Mahatmyam Madhyama Charitra in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Key verses of Mahishasura Mardini story from Durga Saptashati Chapters 2-4.
relatedSearches: Devi Mahatmyam Madhyama Charitra Sanskrit Hindi PDF
Madhyama Charitra recitation rules viniyog Navratri
Tatasta Devi Sarva-devanam Tejobhih Samalankrita verse
Mahalakshmi 18 weapons each god gave Devi list
Madhyama Charitra benefits for victory enemies
Devi Mahatmyam middle episode Markandeya Purana
Aparajita Stuti Narayani Stuti Madhyama Charitra

seoFaqs.question: What is the Devi Mahatmyam Madhyama Charitra?
seoFaqs.answer: The Devi Mahatmyam Madhyama Charitra (देवी माहात्म्यम् मध्यम चरित्रम् — 'the Middle Episode') is the central section of the Devi Mahatmya (also called Durga Saptashati or Chandi Path) preserved in chapters 2, 3 and 4 of the Markandeya Purana. It narrates the second great Devi-charitra: the manifestation of Mahalakshmi from the combined tejas of all the gods to slay the buffalo-demon Mahishasura, who had defeated Indra and the devas and usurped the three worlds. The presiding deity (devata) of this charitra is Mahalakshmi, the rishi is Vishnu (some traditions Markandeya), the chhand is Ushnik, and the bija is Hrim. The charitra covers Devi's birth from the divine tejas, the gifting of weapons by each deity, the slaughter of Mahishasura's army, the shape-shifting battle with Mahishasura himself, and the celebrated Shakradi Stuti (Aparajita Stuti) by Indra and the devas. (Source: Markandeya Purana, Devi Mahatmya chapters 2-4.)

seoFaqs.question: How was Devi Mahalakshmi born to slay Mahishasura?
seoFaqs.answer: After Mahishasura defeated Indra and conquered svarga, the devas led by Brahma approached Vishnu and Shiva. Hearing their plea, intense tejas burst from the faces of all the gods. From Shiva's tejas came Devi's face, from Vishnu's her arms, from Brahma's her feet, from Yama's her chest, from the Sun her toe-radiance, from the moon-gods her breasts, from Kubera her nose, from Prajapati her teeth, from Agni her three eyes, from the Sandhyas her brows, from Vayu her ears, from Varuna her hips and thighs, from Bhumi-devi her loins. Each god then gifted his supreme weapon: Shiva his trishul, Vishnu his Sudarshana chakra, Varuna his shankha, Agni a shakti spear, Vayu his bow and arrows, Indra his vajra and Airavata's bell, Yama his kala-danda, Brahma his akshamala and kamandalu, Kala his sword and shield, Vishvakarma his parashu, the ocean a chudamani, kundalas, keyura and graiveyaka. The eighteen-armed Devi mounted her lion and began the cosmic battle. (Source: Devi Mahatmya 2.9-30.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Madhyama Charitra be recited for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: The Madhyama Charitra is recited daily during all nine days of Sharad Navratri and Chaitra Navratri, with special emphasis on Maha Ashtami (Durga Ashtami) and Maha Navami when Mahishasura was slain — culminating on Vijayadashami (Dussehra), the celebration of Devi's victory. It is also recited on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Friday (Shukravar), on every Ashtami and Navami, on Magha Purnima, on Lakshmi Jayanti, on Kojagiri Purnima (sacred to Mahalakshmi), and on Diwali. The classical Saptashati paath schedule prescribes Prathama Charitra on day 1, Madhyama Charitra (chapters 2-4) on days 2-3, and Uttara Charitra on the remaining days, with the full paath read across all nine nights. The recitation should follow the Argala, Kilaka, Kavach and dhyana of Mahalakshmi, ideally before a Devi yantra or murti with red flowers, kumkum and a ghee diya. (Sources: Devi Mahatmya paath vidhi; Drikpanchang Navratri.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the significance of each god's weapon being given to Mahalakshmi?
seoFaqs.answer: The weapon-gifting episode (ayudha-dana) is the cosmic theological foundation of Devi worship — it teaches that Devi is sarva-deva-mayi (composed of all gods) and sarva-shakti-mayi (the wielder of all divine powers). Each weapon represents the specific function (adhikara) that god surrenders to her: Shiva's trishul is the power of laya (dissolution), Vishnu's chakra is the power of stithi (preservation) and dharma-rakshana, Brahma's kamandalu is creation and tapasya, Indra's vajra is invincibility, Varuna's shankha is the cosmic Om-pranava, Agni's shakti is purifying fire, Yama's danda is dharmic punishment, Vayu's bow is movement and life-breath, Surya's rays are illumination, the ocean's ornaments are abundance and beauty. The episode declares the central Shakta theological truth: every deva's power is in fact a portion of the one Adi-Shakti, who is now reclaiming her own integrated svarupa to slay the demon. (Source: Devi Mahatmya chapter 2; Devi Bhagavata.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Aparajita Stuti / Narayani Stuti from this charitra?
seoFaqs.answer: After Devi slays Mahishasura at the end of the Madhyama Charitra (and again after the Shumbha-Nishumbha vadha at the end of the Uttara Charitra), the devas offer the great Aparajita Stuti — also called Narayani Stuti — beginning with Sarva-mangala-mangalye Shive Sarvartha-sadhike, sharanye tryambake Gauri Narayani namostu te. The stuti is a 24-verse hymn praising Devi as the supreme cosmic mother — the power of creation, preservation and destruction; the supporter of dharma; the slayer of demons; the boon-giver to devotees; the granter of moksha. It contains some of the most quoted verses of all Hindu liturgy — Sharanagata-dinarta-paritrana-parayane and Sristhi-sthithi-vinashanam shakti-bhute Sanatani. The stuti is recited in every Devi temple at the conclusion of arati, and as a standalone Aparajita Stotram for victory in any undertaking. (Source: Devi Mahatmya 4.10-26 and 11.10-30.)

seoFaqs.question: How does Devi defeat Mahishasura in his shape-shifting battle?
seoFaqs.answer: Mahishasura, an asura who had received a boon that no man or god could kill him, took multiple forms to fight Devi. First he came as the buffalo (mahisha) form he was named for, throwing mountains with his horns. Devi caught his neck in a noose. Mahisha then transformed into a lion (simha), which Devi beheaded with her sword. He emerged as a sword-wielding warrior (purusha), then as a great elephant (gaja) that pulled mountains with its trunk, which Devi cut down with her sword. Returning to buffalo form he kicked up the three worlds with his hoofs. Finally Devi, seated on her lion, pinned him with her foot, pierced him with her spear, and as he tried to emerge half-out from the buffalo's mouth in his original asura form, she beheaded him with her great sword (mahasi). The devas rained flowers; the worlds were freed. This iconography became the famous Mahishasuramardini murti seen in every Durga temple. (Source: Devi Mahatmya 3.20-43.)
